actors.name: Jordan Bardella
actors.role: President of the National Rally (Rassemblement National), France
actors.blurb: Bardella remains a dominant figure in French right-wing politics, driving multiple news cycles with his policy stances and personal revelations. His recent interviews and statements on taxation, EU flags, public broadcasting privatization, and his relationship with Maria-Carolina de Bourbon des Deux-Siciles have kept him in the spotlight. Bardella's positions on fuel prices, superprofits taxation, and France's role in the Middle East conflict are central to current political debates.

actors.name: Péter Magyar
actors.blurb: Magyar is shaping the news cycle in Hungary with his efforts to unlock EU funds and reform the country's political and economic landscape. His meetings with EU leaders, promises to abolish controversial institutions like the Sovereignty Protection Office, and plans to release classified documents have generated significant coverage. Magyar's actions are seen as pivotal in Hungary's shift away from Viktor Orbán's policies.

actors.blurb: Trump continues to dominate global right-wing news coverage with his policies, personal statements, and international engagements. His recent interactions with King Charles III, threats to Iran, and plans to feature his image on U.S. passports have kept him at the center of political discourse. Trump's administration's actions on immigration, judicial appointments, and foreign policy also drive significant media attention.

Meloni's stance on immigration, labor reforms, and her defense of controversial judicial decisions also drive significant media attention.
